title: Reflogs, Reachability Windows, and Recovery · Command Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs reflogs, reachability windows, and recovery from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain per-ref and HEAD reflogs, selector syntax, creation policy, worktree scope, expiration categories, and configuration.
Correlate ref updates, checkout history, operation messages, timestamps, actors, and graph evidence without treating reflog order as commit ancestry.
Discover lost tips with reflog, fsck, object inspection, and known content while avoiding commands that may expire or prune candidates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Case G96 is a review in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it.

Inspect the Gita Expiration Retention Policy trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design reflog expiration, gc grace, backup, and incident policy from collaboration and compliance needs without promising archival durability.

content.text: For Gita Expiration Retention Policy, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Gita Expiration Retention Policy precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G96 is a review in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Increasing reflog expiry converts local Git storage into a verified backup.

Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Gita Expiration Retention Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G14 is a review in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design reflog expiration, gc grace, backup, and incident policy from collaboration and compliance needs without promising archival durability.
